chunks.text: \chapter{Cory Named Account Policy}\label{namedch} {\it The information in this section is also available on-line if you have an account on an Instructional machine. See the file /usr/pub/Named.Account.Policy for the most current information. } \se

nt.Policy for the most current information. } \section{Information about the EECS Named Accounts on Cory} \label{named} \index{cory accounts} \index{accounts} \index{accounts!cory} \index{named accounts} \noindent $\Rightarrow$ {\em What is a ``name

nts} \noindent $\Rightarrow$ {\em What is a ``named account''?} A Cory {\em named account} provides an EECS student with a long-term individual computer account on a networked UNIX system. The account is primarily for the use of the email

The account is primarily for the use of the email and network news utilities and has a limited disk allocation. The account is accessible from ASCII terminals in Cory Hall, through modems and over the Internet. Laser and line printers are av

er the Internet. Laser and line printers are available in Cory Hall. Tape backup is performed daily and some UNIX consulting by email is provided. A {\em named account} differs from a {\em class account} because it is long-term and is given a

s account} because it is long-term and is given a login that usually resembles the user's name. A class account is assigned for just one semester and is given a login that resembles the class number. {\em Named accounts} are distributed from 391 Co

. {\em Named accounts} are distributed from 391 Cory Hall; {\em class accounts} are distributed only by instructors. \noindent $\Rightarrow$ {\em Who is qualified to have a named account?} These groups of EECS students are eligible for Co

groups of EECS students are eligible for Cory {\em named accounts:} \begin{itemize} \item undergraduates who are declared L\&S CS majors or EECS majors; \item CS and EE graduate students; \item CS Reentry students; \item EECS and L\&S C

; \item CS Reentry students; \item EECS and L\&S CS majors who graduated at the end of the previous semester. \end{itemize} \noindent $\Rightarrow$ \mbox{\em When/where/how much do I pay?} \index{accounts!payment for named} Payment is due at the be

counts!payment for named} Payment is due at the beginning of the Fall and Spring semesters. The fee is \$15 per semester or any portion of a semester. Accounts paid for at the beginning of a semester will be good through the beginning

a semester will be good through the beginning of the following semester, so payment in the Spring will hold the account over the summer. Fees may be paid for one semester in advance, so that a user may pay \$30 to maintain the account for a

a user may pay \$30 to maintain the account for a calendar year. Graduating seniors may retain an account for one semester after their final semester. Payment is made to the receptionist in 391 Cory when the account form is claimed. Payment by

ry when the account form is claimed. Payment by check or money order should be made payable to {\bf ``UC Regents''}. \pagebreak \noindent $\Rightarrow$ {\em What if I don't get a Cory account?} We would like all EECS students to be reachable by

We would like all EECS students to be reachable by email through Cory, whether or not they have Cory accounts, so we provide a {\bf free email forwarding service}. We maintain qualified EECS users in the ``alias'' file at their requests. Req

in the ``alias'' file at their requests. Requests should be sent to {\bf ``root@cory''}. A UCB class account will not be accepted as an email forwarding address. \noindent $\Rightarrow$ {\em How do I claim my named account?} {\bf New under

How do I claim my named account?} {\bf New undergraduate accounts:} Accounts for newly-qualified undergraduates are available no sooner than the third week of classes each semester. A list of eligible students will be posted outside of rooms 12

ble students will be posted outside of rooms 121 and 301 Cory. If you are on the list, you may claim your account form by going to room 391 during business hours. Bring your student ID card and your payment. An undergraduate who became q

d and your payment. An undergraduate who became qualified in a previous semester but has never claimed the account form may do so at any time by bringing his/her student ID card and payment to room 391 during business hours. In this case, these

391 during business hours. In this case, these rules apply: \begin{itemize} \item payment after January 1 will provide an account from that time through the beginning of the following Fall; \item payment on or after June 1 will provide an accou

m payment on or after June 1 will provide an account from that time through the beginning of the following Spring. \end{itemize} {\bf New graduate student accounts:} CS and EE graduate students who are entering the program will be given free Cory na

e entering the program will be given free Cory named accounts (paid for by the Department) for their first calendar year of attendance. These accounts may be claimed from the EE Graduate Matters Office (207 Cory) or the CS Graduate Office

tters Office (207 Cory) or the CS Graduate Office (367 Soda Hall) about 2 weeks before the start of the semester. {\bf Continuing graduate student accounts:} A graduate student who is beyond the first year may maintain a Cory account at his/h

e first year may maintain a Cory account at his/her own expense. {\bf Renewing active accounts:} Payment to renew an account is due during the beginning of each semester. We will post notices giving information about the date this payment is requ

ng information about the date this payment is required each semester. {\bf Renewing lapsed accounts:} If payment has not been received by the deadline, the account will expire. Once an existing account has expired due to non-payment, these rules

ccount has expired due to non-payment, these rules apply for reinstating service: \begin{itemize} \item for a student who has been {\em continuously enrolled in the major} since the payment first lapsed, payment must be made for the semester for wh

psed, payment must be made for the semester for which payment was neglected. In addition, a \$10 fee will be added to pay for the restoration of the account. Restoration may take up to 2 weeks. \item for a student who returns after havi

s. \item for a student who returns after having {\em withdrawn from school,} been {\em dropped from the major} or been in the {\em co-op program,} payment of the fee for the current semester is all that will be required to get an account restor

ll that will be required to get an account restored. \end{itemize} \noindent $\Rightarrow$ {\em Where do I go for help about a named account?} If you have trouble with your account, contact a member of the EECS Instructional group by sending e

ber of the EECS Instructional group by sending email to {\bf ``root@cory''} or visiting {\bf 384 Cory Hall}. Tutorial help and guidance about UNIX, the terminals and printers is also available from the {\bf XCF} group of student volunteers
ble from the {\bf XCF} group of student volunteers in 117 Cory.
